title: What would actually make you trust an AI-generated strategy enough?
author: Important_Buy626
selftext:
Not hypothetically what would the bar actually have to look like? Specific out-of-sample performance metrics? Explainability of the logic? A certain amount of live paper trading first?

I keep going back and forth on this. Part of me thinks if the logic is sound and the backtest is robust, the source of the strategy shouldn't matter. Another part of me thinks I need to understand why a trade is being made before I let anything run unsupervised.

Where do you actually land on this?

comments.author: mdawe1
comments.body: I ran for 3 weeks on paper and did a shit ton of back tests and then let it loose on the market with an amount of money big enough to do things but not back breaking. If it continues to out perform SPY then I will add more. I was curious about a model that is tuned to make xxx amount per day and xxx per week and as it gets closer more conservative and further away more aggressive. But that mode is just a concept
